behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff: three source collectors must all feed the summary input payload. MOCK-MISS applies because a single-function unit test can pass while wiring drops one source. REAL-INFRA does not force System here: this behavior can be validated with in-process stubs for the three source clients and assertion on the composed payload. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP do not apply because this behavior checks pre-summary input assembly, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real collection/orchestration modules together with stubbed source adapters for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log; execute one report run and assert the exact object sent to the summary step contains fields from all three sources.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure is orchestration/state propagation across modules: collector failure must be transformed into section-level fallback text while pipeline continues. Unit is too low because the bug often appears at module boundaries (exception mapping + report assembly). System is not required: unavailability can be reproduced with an in-process failing adapter. Agentic/Workflow do not apply because correctness does not depend on model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run report orchestration with two normal source adapters and one adapter that raises the same typed error used for source outage; assert run completes, output contains `data unavailable` for only that section, and no global abort occurs.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: As written, this behavior targets summary-content correctness given input data, which can be tested at the summarizer function/object boundary with fixed fixtures. Rejection gate: Agentic was considered, but behavior text does not state a real AI/ML model call; without that concrete evidence, higher level is disqualified. If implementation actually uses an LLM, this should be upgraded to Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the summary generator deterministic fixture data with known key values and assert the produced narrative includes the correct values/facts (and no contradictory values).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Consistent only if summary generation is deterministic/template-based; upgrade to Agentic if a real LLM produces the narrative.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA is mandatory: this behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox over real email infrastructure. In-process mocks would hide real failures (SMTP/API auth, transport, provider acceptance, mailbox delivery). No LLM dependency is required by behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are not minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report pipeline against reachable test instances/endpoints and a real test mailbox; assert the email is received by the configured recipient with expected report body/attachments within an acceptable time window.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses external-service failure handling plus persistence across runs. MOCK-HIDE risk is high: mocked send failures may not match real unreachable conditions/timeouts and can miss retry-path bugs. It also validates run-to-run behavior tied to scheduling/execution lifecycle, which is beyond a single in-process unit boundary. Rejection gate for Integration: behavior text explicitly includes 'email delivery service is unreachable' and 'retried on the next scheduled run,' so real infra/lifecycle concerns are not disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script in an environment where email endpoint is actually unreachable (or blackholed), verify report is persisted to local fallback file; then on the next scheduled/executed run with email restored, verify the saved report is retried and successfully delivered.
